ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for 'Nama Chocolate Drink'; mapped to closest cocoa-based beverage class. L-62 is a Singapore HPB catalogue code for a sweetened cocoa drink.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW covers cocoa and chocolate-based beverages; used as closest defensible match for this sweetened cocoa drink.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific sweetened cocoa beverage product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Nama Chocolate Drink is approximately 250 g (one cup), as commonly served in Singapore food courts and canteens.

nutrient_source.title: L-62 Nama Chocolate — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo record L-62 Nama Chocolate (cr_id G00011012). All per-100g values are as reported by HPB with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for HPB L-62 Nama Chocolate. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: sweetened cocoa/chocolate milk drink with similar sugar content (~10g/100g) and negligible fibre, referenced from University of Sydney GI Database entries for chocolate-flavoured milk drinks and cocoa beverages (GI range 34–68 for chocolate milk; 65 used as conservative estimate for a sugar-dominant, low-fat, low-protein cocoa drink). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 25.9) / 100 ≈ 16.8 for a 250g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar (9.94g/100g) with negligible fibre (0g) and very low potassium (5mg/100g); sodium is low at 3mg/100g but the AHA empty-calorie sugar threshold is triggered
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is very low (3mg/100g), the high sugar content (9.94g/100g) with zero dietary fibre and negligible potassium (5mg/100g) meets the AHA empty-calorie sugar gate for hypertension risk; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption and choose unsweetened cocoa alternatives with higher potassium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94g/100g) with zero dietary fibre results in rapid glucose absorption and significant postprandial glycaemic excursion; estimated GI of 65 (medium-high for a beverage) and GL of 16.8 per 250g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With nearly 10g of sugar per 100g, zero dietary fibre to moderate absorption, and an estimated glycaemic load of 16.8 per standard serving, this drink is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and should be replaced with unsweetened cocoa or water-based beverages.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar (9.94g/100g) with negligible fat (0.25g/100g) and zero fibre; low sodium and cholesterol are favourable but the sugar-driven empty-calorie profile raises CVD risk through triglyceride elevation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low fat (0.25g/100g) and negligible cholesterol (0mg/100g) are favourable for cardiovascular health, but the high added sugar content (9.94g/100g) with zero fibre can elevate triglycerides and contribute to metabolic syndrome risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of sugar-sweetened beverages including this product.

llm_training.natural_language_description: L-62 Nama Chocolate (HPB code G00011012) is a sweetened cocoa-based beverage sold in Singapore food courts, canteens, and hawker centres. The drink is prepared by dissolving cocoa powder and sugar in water, producing a thin, mildly chocolatey liquid with a sweet taste and light body. It is not a thick hot chocolate or a dairy-based chocolate milk; rather, it is a diluted, sugar-forward cocoa drink with very low fat (0.25g/100g), negligible protein (0g/100g), and no dietary fibre. Per 100g it provides 42 kcal, 10.36g carbohydrate of which 9.94g is sugar, 3mg sodium, and 5mg potassium. The near-absence of protein and fibre means glucose from the sugar is absorbed rapidly, making this a high-glycaemic-load beverage when consumed in a standard 250g serving (estimated GL ≈ 16.8). Clinically, this drink is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid blood glucose elevation, and raises moderate concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease through its high added-sugar, empty-calorie profile despite its low sodium content. It is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner as an accompaniment to hawker meals in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Nama Chocolate drink?
What is the sugar content of L-62 chocolate drink?
Is Nama Chocolate suitable for hypertension?
What is 大-62 Nama 巧克力?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011012, confidence 0.95) which is an approved primary source for Singapore food records. All per-100g values are as reported by HPB. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence) but is documented with full reasoning from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo record G00011012; this is a diluted cocoa-sugar-water beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients at detectable levels — confirmed zero per HPB source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; the beverage contains no fibre-contributing ingredients. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source (0.0mg/100g). cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source; the product contains no animal-derived fat. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in HPB Foodo for this record. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported in HPB Foodo for this beverage record as of 2026. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened cocoa and chocolate-flavoured drinks; no direct GI measurement exists for HPB L-62 Nama Chocolate. The input record classified this as food_group 'Mixed' and food_subgroup 'Customisable mixed dishes'; reclassified to 'Beverages' / 'Chocolate and cocoa-based beverages' based on the nutritional profile (42 kcal/100g, 10.36g carbohydrate, 0.25g fat, 0g protein) which is consistent with a diluted sweetened beverage rather than a solid mixed dish. The input health_suitability entries referenced 'Sugar-sweetened beverage' cautions which are consistent with this reclassification.
